assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic Grouping section shows 'Strategy: by-repository' and the table lists 2 epics: 'Epic 1 | TC-9006: trustify-backend | trustify-backend' and 'Epic 2 | TC-9006: trustify-ui | trustify-ui'.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic Grouping table shows Epic 1 summary as 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' and Epic 2 summary as 'TC-9006: trustify-ui', both matching the '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' format exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Task Assignment table: Tasks 1-3 (trustify-backend) are assigned to 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' Epic; Tasks 4-7 (trustify-ui) are assigned to 'TC-9006: trustify-ui' Epic. The 'additional_fields for Tasks' section specifies parent as '{"parent": {"key": "<assigned-epic-key>"}}' (Epic key from sub-step 6a.0), confirming tasks inherit their Epic parent. summary-comment.md corroborates this assignment.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Incorporates links from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks) — evidenced by link decisions in the impact map or task output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Issue Links section under 'Feature "incorporates" Epics': 'TC-9006 incorporates Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend)' and 'TC-9006 incorporates Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui)'. Links are from Feature to Epics only, not to individual Tasks.

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Task Assignment table: backend tasks are numbered 1, 2, 3; frontend tasks are numbered 4, 5, 6, 7. Task Dependencies section confirms frontend tasks depend on backend tasks: 'Task 4 depends on Task 1, Task 2', 'Task 5 depends on Task 4', 'Task 6 depends on Task 4', 'Task 7 depends on Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6'. All backend tasks have strictly lower numbers than dependent frontend tasks.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-exempt tasks (1,2,4,5,6) all contain: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 3 (testing task 'remediation-integration-tests') is exempt but still includes all sections. Task 7 (documentation task 'remediation-documentation') is exempt from Files to Modify/Create/Implementation Notes and correctly includes Repository (trustify-ui), Target Branch (main), Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Epic creation with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key — evidenced by Epic creation parameters in the impact map or output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Type-to-Role Mapping table: '| Epic | Epic | (discovered dynamically) | 1 |' — issue type name is 'Epic' at hierarchy level 1. Parent field section for Epics states: '{"parent": {"key": "TC-9006"}}' — parent is the feature issue key TC-9006.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files contain a '## Repository' section with exactly one repo name: Tasks 1-3 have 'trustify-backend'; Tasks 4-7 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files contain: '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1 through task-7 description files.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: Digest files exist for all 7 tasks (task-1-digest.md through task-7-digest.md), but none contains an actual SHA-256 hash. All use placeholders: 'sha256-md:<64-char-hex-digest>' and '<tagged-digest-from-step-3>' instead of a real 64-character hex string. The assertion explicitly requires 'not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string'. No actual computed digest appears in any output file.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention annotations across tasks 1-6 follow the prescribed format. Examples: Task 1 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/remediation/endpoints/summary.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` module file scope.'; Task 4 'Applies: task modifies `src/api/rest.ts` matching the convention's `.ts` scope.'; Task 5 'Applies: task creates `src/pages/RemediationDashboardPage/RemediationDashboardPage.tsx` matching the convention's `.tsx` page directory scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales are used.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Inherited Field Values table: 'Priority | Major | Feature has a defined priority (not "Undefined") | Yes'. additional_fields for both Epics and Tasks include '"priority": {"name": "Major"}'. summary-comment.md confirms: 'Priority | Major | Yes -- propagated to all Epics and Tasks (Feature priority is "Major", not "Undefined")'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Inherited Field Values table: 'Fix Versions | RHTPA 1.5.0 | Feature has fixVersions set; no `fixVersion scope` setting in Jira Field Defaults (defaults to "both") | Yes'. additional_fields for Epics and Tasks both include '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]'. summary-comment.md confirms: 'Fix Versions | RHTPA 1.5.0 | Yes -- propagated to all Epics and Tasks (Feature has fixVersions set; no fixVersion scope restriction in Jira Field Defaults, defaults to "both")'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an 'Inherited Field Values' table showing: 'Priority | Major | Yes -- propagated to all Epics and Tasks' and 'Fix Versions | RHTPA 1.5.0 | Yes -- propagated to all Epics and Tasks'. Additionally, impact-map.md contains a detailed 'Inherited Field Values' section with the same information plus JSON additional_fields examples.
